content: I want to explore Northeast how should I plan it

**📍 Must-See Spots**
You're going to love **Acadia National Park** in Maine, with its rugged coastline and stunning fall foliage. Don't miss the **Chapel of the Holy Cross** in Massachusetts, a quirky chapel built into the side of a mountain. Visit **The High Line** in New York City, an elevated park built on an old rail line. If you're looking for something offbeat, head to **The Berkshires** in Massachusetts for a mix of art, culture, and outdoor adventures.

**🍜 Local Food & Drinks**
You have to try a **New England Clam Chowder**, a creamy soup made with fresh clams and potatoes. **Lobster Rolls** are a must-try, with succulent lobster meat served on a toasted bun. **Apple Cider Donuts** are a fall favorite, and **Maine Blueberries** are a sweet treat. Wash it all down with a **Samuel Adams** beer from Boston.

**📅 Best Time to Go**
The Northeast is amazing in all seasons, but it really depends on what you like. Summer is perfect for beach trips and outdoor adventures. Fall is stunning with foliage, while winter offers skiing and cozy vibes. Spring brings blooming flowers and festivals.

What's your vibe - are you more into exploring cities, or do you prefer outdoor adventures?
I meant north east of india

**📍 Must-See Spots**
You're going to love **Tawang** in Arunachal Pradesh - its stunning monastery is a must-visit. **Shillong** in Meghalaya is a charming hill station with a vibrant culture. For an offbeat experience, head to **Ziro Valley** in Arunachal Pradesh, known for its picturesque landscapes and rich tribal heritage. If you're up for an adventure, explore the **Dibru-Saikhowa National Park** in Assam for its breathtaking wildlife and scenic beauty.

**🍜 Local Food & Drinks**
Be sure to try the iconic **Jadoh** (a hearty rice dish) and **Smoked Pork** in Meghalaya. In Assam, indulge in **Masoor Tenga** (a tangy fish curry) and wash it down with a cup of **Assam Tea**. Don't miss the **Thukpa** (a warming noodle soup) in Arunachal Pradesh.

**📅 Best Time to Go**
The best time to visit North East India is from October to April, when the weather is pleasant and ideal for outdoor activities. Summer brings lush green landscapes, while winter offers a glimpse into the region's snow-covered beauty. Monsoon, though, can make some areas inaccessible.

What's your idea of adventure - trekking, wildlife spotting, or exploring hidden villages?
I think kind of all, but not too touristy

**📍 Must-See Spots**
You're going to love **Tawang** in Arunachal Pradesh - its stunning monastery is a must-visit! **Shillong** in Meghalaya is a charming hill station with a beautiful lake and lush forests. For an offbeat experience, head to **Ziro Valley** in Arunachal Pradesh, known for its scenic beauty and rich tribal culture. If you're up for an adventure, visit **Dibrugarh** in Assam, a great base for exploring the surrounding tea gardens and forests.

**🍜 Local Food & Drinks**
Be sure to try the famous **Momos** (Tibetan dumplings) and **Thukpa** (noodle soup) in Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh. In Assam, indulge in **Masor Tenga** (fish curry) and wash it down with a cup of **Assam Tea**. 

**📅 Best Time to Go**
The best time to visit North East India is from October to April, with comfortable temperatures and minimal rainfall. Summer brings lush green landscapes, while winter offers a chance to experience festivals like the **Losar** in Sikkim. Monsoon season (June-September) can be challenging, but it's great for trekking and photography.

What's your idea of an ideal adventure - trekking, rafting, or exploring hidden villages?
ideal adventure would be trekking exploring villages and mostly int he woods

**📍 Must-See Spots**
You're in for a treat! **Tawang** in Arunachal Pradesh is a stunning monastery town with breathtaking Himalayan views. **Shillong** in Meghalaya is a charming hill station with lush forests and vibrant markets. **Kaziranga National Park** in Assam is a UNESCO World Heritage Site for incredible rhino and tiger spotting. For something offbeat, try **Ziro Valley** in Arunachal Pradesh for its picturesque landscape and Apatani culture.

**🍜 Local Food & Drinks**
Get ready to indulge in some amazing flavors! Try the **Momos** (Tibetan dumplings) in Sikkim, **Jadoh** (a traditional Khasi dish) in Meghalaya, and **Masoor Tenga** (a sour fish curry) in Assam. Wash it down with a cup of **Chhurpi** (a type of Tibetan tea) or **Rice Beer** in Meghalaya.

**📅 Best Time to Go**
The best time to visit North East India is from October to April, when the weather is pleasant and clear. Summer (May-June) brings lush greenery, while monsoon (July-September) offers a unique, misty experience. Winters (December-February) can be chilly, but perfect for snow and festivals.

What's your adventure style: trekking, wildlife exploration, or immersing in local culture?
